title: Updating Variables 🔄
description: Change the data stored inside your variables.

slides.title: Things Change 🔄
slides.content:
Why are they called "Variables"? Because they can **vary** (change)!

Once a variable is created, you can swap out the data inside the box for new data.

slides.title: Updating 🛠️
slides.content:
To update a variable, you just use its name and the `=` sign again. 

**CRITICAL**: You DO NOT write the data type keyword again!

```java
int ammo = 30; // Declare AND Initialize

// Player shoots a bullet!
ammo = 29; // UPDATE - Notice I didn't write 'int' again
```

quizData.question: What is the correct way to update an existing variable named 'lives'?
quizData.explanation: When updating, you just use the established name and the equals sign. Writing 'int' again creates an error because Java thinks you're creating a new box with the same name.

slides.title: Math in Updates 🧮
slides.content: Often, you want to update a variable based on its *current* value. You can do math on the right side of the `=` sign!

quizData.question: If `int hp = 100;`, what will `hp` equal after running `hp = hp - 20;`?
quizData.explanation: The right side `100 - 20` resolves to 80. Then 80 is stored back into the `hp` box.
